rejected_candidates.text: Although statins have reached the status of preferred treatment for hyperlipidemia
rejected_candidates.reason: No explicit evidence that statins treat hyperlipidemia in this chunk

rejected_candidates.text: There are reasons to consider other medications.
rejected_candidates.reason: This statement is general and does not specify a relationship between drugs and conditions.

rejected_candidates.text: Some of the therapeutic options are listed in Table 6.
rejected_candidates.reason: No specific drug-therapy or drug-condition relationships mentioned here.

rejected_candidates.text: A number of studies have looked at comparisons of drug combinations.
rejected_candidates.reason: No explicit evidence linking drugs to conditions or biomarkers.

rejected_candidates.text: Some reviews have shown modest reduction of plasma lipids with the use of substances such as garlic, artichoke leaf extract, nuts, plant stanolols, psyllium, soluble fiber, orange juice, and red yeast rice.
rejected_candidates.reason: These statements are about complementary products, not statins or other drugs.

rejected_candidates.text: A major deficiency in the study of alternative products is the lack of studies that use hard cardiovascular endpoints instead of a surrogate marker, such as changes in LDL-C.
rejected_candidates.reason: This statement discusses deficiencies in research methods and does not establish drug-condition relationships.
